calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jul-24-2015 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-23-2014 Set For Trial On Aug-24-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-27-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Patrick Connolly, Esq., 415.357.0600, for plaintiff Rocky Point Partners, LLC.
Jonathan Sommer, Esq., 415.981.0550, for defendants Yat-Pang Au, Russian Hill Group, LLC, Nob Hill Vistas, LLC, Eureka Park LLC, Yat-Hon Au, Yat-Gai Au, Parnassus Place, LLC and Russian Hill Associates, LLC.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU, PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC, RUSSIAN HILL ASSOCIATES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the causes of action for breach of contract and common counts, the demurrer is overruled. "A licensed broker may be able under appropriate circumstances to recover under an oral agreement or in quantum meruit for certain services other than the purchase, sale, or leasing of real property." (Phillippe v. Shapell Industries (1987) 43 Cal.3d 1247, 1255.) "A valid oral argument could be made for the special services alleged, consisting of the surveys made, the furnishing of plans, specifications and estimates'" (Owen v. Nat'l Container Corp. of Cal. (1952) 115 Cal.App.2d 21, 26.) The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges that Telegraph Hill Properties performed services beyond negotiating and effecting a purchase of real property. The SAC alleges various building and approval services performed by Plaintiff, including developing plans for improvement, obtaining bids for the improvement work, assisting in the selection of architects and engineers, obtaining permits, hiring and consulting with land surveyors, and preparing budgets. (SAC, pars. 20-21.) As to the cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as unopposed. Additionally, the Order sustaining the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint gave Plaintiff leave to amend to plead facts with the requisite specificity for a fraud claim. Plaintiff added no additional facts to support this cause of action in the SAC. As to Defendants' request for judicial notice, the Court takes judicial notice of the existence of the documents pursuant to Evid. Code sec. 452(d), but does not judicially notice the content of the documents. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/mjm

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-16-2014 Continued To Aug-06-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Continued To Jun-04-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS RUSSIAN HILL ASSOCIATES, LLC, YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU and PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with ten days leave to amend. The Court does not rule on Defendants' request for judicial notice since it has been withdrawn. (Reply Brief at pg. 4, line 27- pg. 5, line 14.) As to the first cause of action, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") fails to plead facts establishing that it is not barred by the statute of frauds. (Civ. Code sec. 1624(a)(4).) Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if it can in good faith, that the "services rendered" were those other than listing properties for sale. As to the second cause of action, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend on the same ground, since it seeks the same recovery as the first cause of action. "When a common count is used as an alternative way of seeking the same recovery demanded in a specific cause of action, and is based on the same facts, the common count is demurrable if the cause of action is demurrable." (McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 394.) As to the third cause of action, the FAC does not plead facts with the requisite specificity. (See Lazar v. Sup. Ct. (1996) 12 Cal.4th 631, 645.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the operative pleading and the opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than March 7, 2014 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU AND PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
